meaning: adj.多斑点的,多污点的,质量不一的,发疹的
examples: He quit in 1981 – had a spotty political career.
It turned out to be a spotty globe just like the earth.
As for Intel, their batting average on forecasting even quarterly revenues accurately is truly spotty.
Once you manage to get on the airport Wi-Fi, access is often spotty and slow.
